ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.
M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported
e.g., Self-Report, Parent Form, Short Form, Revised Version, Other Informant Form
e.g., Baseline, 6-Month Follow Up, Year 2, Daily

ElementDescription: Think about whether your child is able to go to school, college or work every day, whether the problem makes them late or miss lessons, and if it affects their concentration or writing when they are trying to study, do exams, or work
Notes: 0=The problem doesn't affect this at all; 2=The problem affects this slightly; 4=The problem definitely affects this; 6=The problem affects this a lot; 8=The problem affects this very severely

ElementDescription: Think about whether your child is able to shower, bath, and dress without help, or if they find organizing time difficult. Would your child be able to help with tasks and chores at home? unit/facility?
Notes: 0=The problem doesn't affect this at all; 2=The problem affects this slightly; 4=The problem definitely affects this; 6=The problem affects this a lot; 8=The problem affects this very severely

ElementDescription: Think about whether your child is able to see friends when they want to, for example going to sleepovers, going shopping or to the cinema with friends, eating in restaurants, or going to after school activities.
Notes: 0=The problem doesn't affect this at all; 2=The problem affects this slightly; 4=The problem definitely affects this; 6=The problem affects this a lot; 8=The problem affects this very severely

ElementDescription: Think about whether your child can do things that they enjoy when they are alone, for example reading, watching TV, playing a musical instrument, and playing computer games.
Notes: 0=The problem doesn't affect this at all; 2=The problem affects this slightly; 4=The problem definitely affects this; 6=The problem affects this a lot; 8=The problem affects this very severely

ElementDescription: Think about whether your child is able to get with the people they live with (parents, brother, or sisters), or spend time with friends or boy/girlfriend. For example, can they go on family holidays, stay over at a friend's house, or go on a date?
Notes: 0=The problem doesn't affect this at all; 2=The problem affects this slightly; 4=The problem definitely affects this; 6=The problem affects this a lot; 8=The problem affects this very severely
